cards.headline: US diesel tops $5 as Iran tensions spike
cards.summary: Average US diesel prices have risen above $5 a gallon, with regular gasoline approaching $4, nearly $1.25 more than a year ago, according to the Guardian. The surge is tied to heightened US-Iran tensions over the Strait of Hormuz driving up crude costs. Heating oil customers are also set to receive compensation following the price spikes.

quiz.prompt: How much more expensive is regular US gasoline compared to a year ago?
quiz.explanation: The Guardian reported regular gas is nearly a dollar and a quarter — about $1.25 — more than a year ago.

cards.headline: Netflix stock falls on disappointing earnings forecast
cards.summary: Netflix shares fell after the company reported mixed earnings and announced it would cut back on the frequency of its 'What We Watched' engagement reports, a move that displeased Wall Street. The streaming giant said it would provide fewer updates on viewing data going forward. Both CNBC and MarketWatch flagged investor frustration with the reduced transparency.

quiz.prompt: Why did Wall Street react negatively to Netflix's latest earnings update?
options: Netflix said it would publish less viewing engagement data
quiz.explanation: Netflix said it would cut back on the frequency of its 'What We Watched' reports, which Wall Street uses to gauge engagement.

cards.headline: UnitedHealth beats estimates, raises earnings outlook
cards.summary: UnitedHealth Group beat analyst estimates and raised its earnings outlook as the healthcare giant works to stabilize margins by shrinking membership and exiting unprofitable contracts, according to CNBC. The company is also pouring $1.5 billion into AI as part of its cost-reining strategy. The strong results come as rival health insurer Elevance's earnings rattled the broader health insurance sector.

quiz.prompt: How much is UnitedHealth investing in AI as part of its turnaround strategy?
quiz.explanation: CNBC reported UnitedHealth is pouring $1.5 billion into AI to help stabilize its margins.

cards.headline: Alphabet shares drop on Gemini delay concerns
cards.summary: Alphabet's stock fell more than 4% after Gemini delays raised concerns that Google is falling further behind rivals in the artificial-intelligence race, according to MarketWatch. Investors interpreted the delays as a sign that the company is struggling to keep pace with competitors. The selloff highlights growing anxiety about which tech giants will lead the next phase of AI development.

quiz.prompt: Why did Alphabet's stock fall more than 4%?
options: A major antitrust ruling against Google
Delays to its Gemini AI product raised competitiveness concerns
A data breach affecting Google Cloud
quiz.explanation: MarketWatch reported that Gemini delays suggested Google is struggling to keep up in the AI race, sending shares down more than 4%.

cards.headline: SpaceX shares slip below IPO debut price
cards.summary: Share trading in Elon Musk's SpaceX has been volatile since the company went public about a month ago, and the stock has now notched its first close below its IPO price, according to BBC and MarketWatch. Analysts describe the success of its megarocket as the company's 'single most important watch item' as it prepares for a crucial upcoming launch. The dip marks a notable moment for one of the most anticipated public listings in recent memory.

quiz.prompt: What milestone did SpaceX's stock recently hit?
options: It doubled from its IPO price
It closed below its IPO price for the first time
It was added to the S&P 500
quiz.explanation: MarketWatch reported SpaceX's stock notched its first close below its IPO price amid volatile trading since going public.

cards.headline: Trump Media launches paid fast-feed for Wall Street traders
cards.summary: Trump Media is launching a premium paid feed giving Wall Street traders early access to its most influential social media posts, according to the BBC. The product is aimed at professional investors who want to react quickly to market-moving statements. The move represents a direct monetisation of the platform's political signal value to financial markets.

quiz.prompt: What is Trump Media's new paid product designed to offer Wall Street traders?
options: Early access to key social media posts
quiz.explanation: The BBC reported Trump Media is launching a fast, paid feed giving traders early access to its most influential posts.

cards.headline: BlackRock shares jump after earnings beat
cards.summary: BlackRock shares rose after the asset management giant reported an earnings beat, with CEO Larry Fink expressing optimism about market strength and momentum, according to YahooFin. However, the firm's crypto assets declined 39% in the second quarter. The results add to a strong earnings season for major US financial institutions.

quiz.prompt: What happened to BlackRock's crypto assets in the second quarter?
quiz.explanation: YahooFin reported BlackRock's crypto assets declined 39% in the second quarter, even as overall earnings beat expectations.

cards.headline: Record home prices deter buyers despite builder discounts
cards.summary: Existing-home prices have hit a new record high while mortgage rates remain elevated and consumer confidence is stressed, contributing to a drop in existing home sales and builder sentiment, according to CNBC. MarketWatch notes that builders in at least 10 US metro areas are slashing prices on new homes to attract buyers. The combination of high prices and high borrowing costs is making this one of the most difficult summers in recent memory for the housing market.

quiz.prompt: What are some builders doing in certain US metro areas to attract buyers?
options: Partnering with the federal government on subsidies
quiz.explanation: MarketWatch reported that builders in 10 US metro areas are cutting prices on new homes as existing-home prices hit record highs.

cards.headline: UK economy returns to growth in May
cards.summary: The UK economy returned to modest growth in May, reversing a slight contraction seen the previous month, according to the BBC. The data offers some relief after a difficult period for the British economy. The figures arrive as the UK also sealed a £5.2 billion trade deal with Switzerland, which includes scrapping roaming charges and allowing British nationals to use e-gates at Swiss airports.

quiz.prompt: What did the UK's May GDP data show?
options: The fastest growth in five years
quiz.explanation: The BBC reported UK GDP returned to modest growth in May, reversing the slight contraction seen the previous month.

cards.headline: Smartphone shipments hit 13-year low in Q2
cards.summary: Global smartphone shipments fell to a 13-year low in the second quarter, with an AI-related memory shortage identified as a key factor hammering the industry, according to YahooFin. The shortage is driven by surging demand for AI chips and memory, which is crowding out supply for consumer devices. The decline underscores how the AI investment boom is creating unexpected knock-on effects across the tech supply chain.

quiz.prompt: What is cited as a key reason for the 13-year low in Q2 smartphone shipments?
options: Weak consumer demand in China only
A major factory fire in Asia
quiz.explanation: YahooFin reported that an AI memory shortage was a key factor hammering smartphone shipments to a 13-year low in Q2.

cards.headline: Scattered Spider hackers jailed for TfL cyberattack
cards.summary: Owen Flowers and Thalha Jubair, two members of the prolific Scattered Spider hacking group, were each sentenced to five and a half years in jail for hacking Transport for London. The attack cost TfL £39 million and resulted in the theft of data belonging to millions of commuters. The pair had pleaded guilty to their roles in the breach.

quiz.prompt: How much did the Transport for London cyberattack cost the organisation?
quiz.explanation: The Guardian reported the attack cost Transport for London £39 million.

cards.headline: SpaceX aborts second Starship V3 launch after ignition
cards.summary: SpaceX suddenly aborted its second Starship V3 launch attempt after ignition, without immediately explaining what went wrong. The company's stock plunged more than 4% in after-hours trading before recovering some losses. The abort adds to a period of volatility for SpaceX shares since the company went public a month ago.

quiz.prompt: By how much did SpaceX stock fall in after-hours trading following the aborted launch?
quiz.explanation: TechCrunch reported SpaceX's stock plunged more than 4% in after-hours trading before paring losses.

cards.headline: xAI sues user for generating child abuse images with Grok
cards.summary: Elon Musk's AI startup xAI has filed one of the first lawsuits by an AI company against a user, accusing a South Carolina man of using Grok to generate child sexual abuse material. The case comes as xAI can no longer deny Grok is capable of producing such content. A separate class-action lawsuit against multiple AI makers over deepfake CSAM has also expanded with new plaintiffs.

quiz.prompt: Who has xAI sued in what is described as one of the first cases of its kind?
options: A South Carolina man accused of using Grok to generate CSAM
A rival AI company for copying its model
A journalist who published leaked Grok outputs
A former employee who stole training data
quiz.explanation: The Guardian reported xAI sued a South Carolina man accused of using Grok to create child sexual abuse material, in one of the first such cases brought by an AI company against a user.

cards.headline: UK proposes midnight social media ban for 16- and 17-year-olds
cards.summary: The UK government is proposing a midnight to 6am social media curfew for teenagers aged 16 and 17, saying it will improve young people's lives. Campaigners have called the plan a missed opportunity, and teenagers surveyed by the Guardian broadly disagreed with aspects of the proposal. A separate psychologist's perspective warns that the current debate around smartphones may obscure deeper issues facing adolescents.

quiz.prompt: What hours would the UK's proposed social media curfew apply to for 16- and 17-year-olds?
quiz.explanation: The BBC and Guardian both reported the proposed curfew would run from midnight to 6am.

cards.headline: AI toys for children: promise and serious peril
cards.summary: AI is rapidly moving beyond chatbots and into toys, dolls, and robots designed to befriend children. A leading child-development expert says the technology offers real promise but also risks crowding out human interaction crucial to healthy development. Separately, UK watchdogs are warning parents about the growing use of AI nudification apps to exploit children's photos.

quiz.prompt: What risk does a leading child-development expert associate with AI companions for children?
options: Crowding out human interaction vital to healthy development
quiz.explanation: NPR reported that a leading child-development expert warned AI companions risk crowding out the human interaction children need for healthy development.

cards.headline: EU orders Google to share search data and open Android AI
cards.summary: The European Union has officially ruled that Google must share its search data with rivals and open up AI on Android devices. Google has warned that the changes could endanger user privacy and security. The decision marks a significant step in the EU's ongoing effort to curb the dominance of major tech platforms.

quiz.prompt: What did Google say about the EU's ruling requiring it to share search data and open Android AI?
options: The changes could endanger user privacy and security
The ruling was fair and it would comply fully
The decision would have no practical effect
quiz.explanation: Ars Technica reported Google warned that the EU-mandated changes could endanger user privacy and security.

cards.headline: Hyundai workers strike over fear of humanoid robots
cards.summary: Workers at a Hyundai auto factory have gone on strike driven by fears over the planned deployment of humanoid robots. Hyundai aims to deploy 25,000 Atlas robots starting with US factories in 2028. The walkout highlights growing labour unrest over automation as robot deployment moves from concept to concrete timelines.

quiz.prompt: How many Atlas robots does Hyundai aim to deploy starting in 2028?
quiz.explanation: Ars Technica reported Hyundai aims to deploy 25,000 Atlas robots beginning with US factories in 2028.

cards.headline: Brain implant lets paralysed man feed himself again
cards.summary: A man paralysed from the chest down after a swimming accident six years ago can now move his arms and hands and feel the sensation of touch, thanks to a double neural bypass and months of training. The breakthrough, reported by the Guardian, allowed Keith Thomas to feed himself and drink from a cup. The case represents a significant advance in brain-computer interface technology for paralysis patients.

quiz.prompt: What procedure allowed Keith Thomas to regain arm movement and touch sensation?
options: A double neural bypass combined with months of training
A robotic exoskeleton linked to eye movements
quiz.explanation: The Guardian reported that a double neural bypass and months of training enabled Keith Thomas to move his arms and feel touch again.

cards.headline: Moroccan insider reveals widespread domestic Pegasus use
cards.summary: A former member of Morocco's domestic intelligence service has come forward as a whistleblower, revealing that the country's internal security services deployed Pegasus spyware from 2017 against key domestic and foreign targets. The disclosure provides rare insider confirmation of how the controversial surveillance tool was used within a country's own borders. The revelations add to international scrutiny of Pegasus deployments worldwide.

quiz.prompt: From what year does the Moroccan whistleblower say Pegasus spyware was deployed internally?
quiz.explanation: The Guardian reported the whistleblower said Morocco's internal security services deployed Pegasus from 2017 against domestic and foreign targets.

cards.headline: Ransomware attack halts Fairlife dairy production in US
cards.summary: Coca-Cola has confirmed that production at its Fairlife dairy unit will remain suspended in the United States following a ransomware attack. The company did not immediately provide details on when operations would resume. The incident underscores the growing vulnerability of food supply chains to cyberattacks.

quiz.prompt: Which Coca-Cola unit had US dairy production suspended after a ransomware attack?
quiz.explanation: TechCrunch reported that Coca-Cola said dairy production at its Fairlife unit would remain suspended following a ransomware hack.

cards.headline: US launches sixth straight night of Iran strikes
cards.summary: The United States has mounted its sixth consecutive night of attacks on Iran, with Iranian media reporting that an airport, a bridge, and a communications tower were targeted. Iran has warned of a wider war, and separately condemned one strike as a 'cowardly war crime' after a blast near a children's cancer hospital. The US also boarded a ship in the Strait of Hormuz as the confrontation continued to escalate.

quiz.prompt: What did Iran warn could follow the continued US strikes?
quiz.explanation: Iranian officials warned that the ongoing US strikes risked escalating into a wider war.

cards.headline: Vance accuses Israel of manipulating US Iran war opinion
cards.summary: US Vice President JD Vance has accused some Israeli politicians of trying to manipulate American public opinion in order to prolong the conflict with Iran. Vance said he believes certain Israeli figures are attempting to derail US efforts to end the fighting. Al Jazeera separately noted that Washington's most difficult partner in the Iran talks may be found within its own alliance.

quiz.prompt: What did JD Vance accuse some Israeli politicians of trying to do?
options: Negotiate a separate ceasefire with Iran
Manipulate US public opinion to prolong the Iran war
Block US arms shipments to Ukraine
Pressure Congress to declare war on Iran
quiz.explanation: Vance said he believes some Israeli politicians are trying to manipulate US public opinion in order to prolong the conflict with Iran.

cards.headline: Trump to deliver primetime election integrity address
cards.summary: President Trump is scheduled to give a primetime address from the White House focused on election security, though details about the speech remain slim. Critics fear Trump may use the address to spread doubt about election integrity ahead of the 2026 midterms. Trump has for years made false claims about the security of American elections.

quiz.prompt: What upcoming event are critics concerned Trump's election integrity address could be aimed at influencing?
quiz.explanation: Critics fear Trump may use the primetime address to sow doubt about election integrity ahead of the 2026 midterm elections.

cards.headline: Zelenskyy defends firing popular defence minister
cards.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has defended his decision to dismiss Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, saying he had to choose 'one side or the other' after a breakdown of relations between the ministry and military leaders. Fedorov was widely credited with advancing Ukraine's drone warfare capabilities and was considered the country's tech-savvy defence innovator. The dismissal has sparked protests and intense political criticism inside Ukraine.

quiz.prompt: What was Fedorov particularly known for during his tenure as Ukraine's defence minister?
quiz.explanation: Fedorov was widely credited with advancing Ukraine's use of drones on the battlefield and was regarded as the country's tech-savvy defence innovator.

cards.headline: Uganda Ebola-free as DRC outbreak worsens sharply
cards.summary: Uganda has discharged its last Ebola patient and begun a 42-day countdown to officially declaring its outbreak over, while also lobbying for travel restrictions to be lifted. In stark contrast, the WHO has warned that the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has expanded faster than any previous outbreak on record. The diverging situations highlight the difficulty of containing the virus across borders.

quiz.prompt: What milestone did Uganda reach in its Ebola situation?
options: It discharged its last Ebola patient
It received a new WHO emergency grant
quiz.explanation: Uganda discharged its last remaining Ebola patient and began the 42-day countdown required to officially declare the outbreak over.

cards.headline: Canadian wildfire smoke triggers US air quality alerts
cards.summary: Smoke from more than 800 wildfires in Canada has spread south, triggering air quality alerts across 20 US states from Minnesota to New York. Wildfires in Ontario made Toronto's air quality the worst in the world, turning the sky over the city yellow. Authorities in both countries urged people to stay indoors as conditions deteriorated.

quiz.prompt: Approximately how many wildfires were burning in Canada as smoke spread into the United States?
quiz.explanation: Smoke from more than 800 Canadian wildfires spread south, triggering air quality alerts across 20 US states.

cards.headline: Venezuela earthquake death toll rises to nearly 5,000
cards.summary: The number of confirmed dead from Venezuela's earthquake has risen to nearly 5,000, with the UN estimating that around 50,000 people are still missing and many more feared dead under rubble. The scale of the disaster has pushed Venezuela's ongoing democratic crisis into the background as the country focuses on recovery. The earthquakes struck with twin tremors that caused widespread destruction.

quiz.prompt: How many people does the UN estimate are still missing following Venezuela's earthquake?
quiz.explanation: The UN estimates approximately 50,000 people are missing following the Venezuela earthquake, with many more feared dead under rubble.

cards.headline: ICE agents still lack body cameras after DHS pledge
cards.summary: The Department of Homeland Security pledged months ago to equip all immigration agents with body cameras, but that commitment has not been fulfilled. In recent days, federal immigration agents fatally shot two immigrant fathers, and none of the officers involved were wearing body cameras. The White House separately confirmed that ICE traffic stops will continue despite the deadly incidents.

quiz.prompt: What was notable about the ICE officers involved in the recent fatal shootings of two immigrant fathers?
options: None of them were wearing body cameras
They had not received use-of-force training
They were acting against direct orders
quiz.explanation: None of the federal immigration officers involved in the fatal shootings of two immigrant fathers were wearing body cameras, despite DHS having pledged to equip all agents with them.

cards.headline: Italian motorway chief jailed over Genoa bridge collapse
cards.summary: Thirty-two people have been convicted over the 2018 collapse of the Morandi Bridge in Genoa, which killed 43 people. Former motorway operator chief Giovanni Castellucci received a 12-year prison sentence, the highest handed down in the case. The verdict follows years of legal proceedings over one of Italy's deadliest modern infrastructure disasters.

quiz.prompt: How many people were killed in the 2018 Genoa Morandi Bridge collapse?
quiz.explanation: The 2018 collapse of the Morandi Bridge in Genoa killed 43 people, leading to the convictions of 32 individuals including former motorway chief Giovanni Castellucci.

cards.headline: White House staffer probed for betting on Trump speeches
cards.summary: A White House teleprompter operator has been accused of using inside knowledge of President Trump's speeches to make nearly $100,000 in bets on the prediction market platform Kalshi. Officials are now investigating the case, which is described as the first known instance of suspected insider trading on a prediction market originating from inside the White House. The staffer is alleged to have used advance access to speech content to place winning trades.

quiz.prompt: Which prediction market platform was allegedly used by the White House staffer to place bets on Trump's speeches?
quiz.explanation: The White House teleprompter operator is accused of using inside knowledge of Trump's speeches to make nearly $100,000 on the prediction market platform Kalshi.

cards.headline: Suber leads Open as McIlroy struggles at Birkdale
cards.summary: American Jackson Suber moved to the top of the leaderboard at five under par after sinking an eagle on the 17th hole in the first round of the Open Championship at Royal Birkdale. Defending champion Scottie Scheffler and Bryson DeChambeau also made encouraging starts, while Rory McIlroy posted a sluggish round of 72. England's Dan Brown led the home contingent as attention shifts to golf following the national football team's World Cup exit.

quiz.prompt: Who led the Open Championship leaderboard after the first round at Royal Birkdale?
quiz.explanation: Jackson Suber moved to the top of the leaderboard on five under par after sinking an eagle on the 17th hole in round one.

cards.headline: Tuchel vows '100%' commitment to England for Euro 2028
cards.summary: Thomas Tuchel says he is fully committed to remaining as England manager and leading the team at Euro 2028, despite fierce criticism over his defensive tactics in the 2-1 World Cup semi-final defeat to Argentina. BBC Sport reports that England players are hotly debating those tactics internally, and reports also suggest Tuchel was unhappy with Kobbie Mainoo's attitude during the tournament. Commentators including Gary Neville argue England's issues run deeper than any one manager, pointing to long-standing structural problems in English football.

quiz.prompt: What tournament has Tuchel pledged to lead England at following the World Cup exit?
quiz.explanation: Tuchel stated he is '100%' committed to continuing as England manager into Euro 2028.

cards.headline: New York issues air quality alert before World Cup final
cards.summary: Officials in New York and New Jersey issued an air quality health alert due to wildfire smoke just days before the World Cup final is set to be played there. Spain and Argentina are preparing to face each other for the sport's biggest prize on Sunday. Predicted lineups and tactics are already being widely discussed as the footballing world turns its attention to the showpiece event.

quiz.prompt: What caused the air quality alert issued near the World Cup final venue?
quiz.explanation: Officials in New York and New Jersey issued the air quality health alert specifically because of wildfire smoke.

cards.headline: Viral Messi-Yamal baby photo captures football's destiny
cards.summary: Photos of Lionel Messi posing with baby Lamine Yamal for a 2007 charity calendar have resurfaced ahead of the World Cup final between Argentina and Spain. The photographer, Joan Monfort, said he had no idea at the time who the baby was. The images have gone viral, with the two players now set to face each other on the world's biggest stage nearly two decades after the pictures were taken.

quiz.prompt: In what year was the charity calendar photo of Messi and baby Lamine Yamal taken?
quiz.explanation: The photographer Joan Monfort took the photos for a charity calendar in 2007, when Lamine Yamal was a baby.

cards.headline: Merlier completes Tour de France hat-trick on stage 12
cards.summary: Tim Merlier of Soudal Quick-Step won stage 12 of the Tour de France 2026, edging out Kooij and Philipsen in what is likely the final sprint stage of this year's race. Tadej Pogacar safely retained the yellow jersey. The victory gave Merlier a hat-trick of stage wins at this year's Tour.

quiz.prompt: How many stage wins did Tim Merlier have at the 2026 Tour de France after stage 12?
quiz.explanation: Stage 12 was described as giving Merlier a hat-trick of stage wins at this year's Tour de France.

cards.headline: Arsenal close in on £34m deal for winger Tzolis
cards.summary: Arsenal have agreed a deal worth up to €40m (£34m) with Club Brugge to sign Greek winger Christos Tzolis, according to multiple reports. The signing is seen as a replacement for Leandro Trossard and comes as the club also faces the need to find defensive cover following a back injury to William Saliba. Surgery is listed as one option for Saliba as Arsenal assess the problem.

quiz.prompt: Which club are Arsenal signing Christos Tzolis from?
quiz.explanation: Arsenal agreed a deal worth up to €40m with Club Brugge to sign Greek winger Christos Tzolis.

cards.headline: Root's 99 not out levels England-India ODI series
cards.summary: Joe Root hit an unbeaten 99 to guide England to a four-wicket win over India in the second one-day international in Cardiff, levelling the three-match series. England recovered after a dismal start that saw them fall to 8-2 in the fourth over, with both openers dismissed cheaply and captain Harry Brook out for 16 after a poor shot. The deciding third ODI now awaits.

quiz.prompt: What score did Joe Root finish on in England's series-levelling ODI win over India?
quiz.explanation: Root finished on 99 not out, just one run short of a century, as England won by four wickets to level the series.

cards.headline: US Soccer coy on Pochettino and post-World Cup direction
cards.summary: US Soccer Federation chief executive JT Batson declined to confirm whether the federation regrets President Trump's involvement in the Folarin Balogun red-card saga, saying only that 'the president is the president.' Batson also confirmed that talks with Mauricio Pochettino over the USMNT head coach role remain active, while the sporting director position remains unfilled following the departure of Brian Crocker. The federation says it will 'take a break' before finalising its long-term structure ahead of the 2030 World Cup.

quiz.prompt: What role at US Soccer remains unfilled following Brian Crocker's exit?
quiz.explanation: The sporting director position at US Soccer was left unfilled after Brian Crocker departed, and Batson confirmed it has not yet been filled.

cards.headline: McGregor's UFC return draws 16 million viewers on Paramount+
cards.summary: Conor McGregor's return at UFC 329 drew nearly 16 million viewers on Paramount+ across the US and Latin America, making it a massive commercial success for the platform. McGregor had previously vowed to fight again after his 69-second return to the octagon. The viewing figures underline the enduring star power of the Irish fighter.

quiz.prompt: Approximately how many viewers watched McGregor's UFC 329 return on Paramount+?
quiz.explanation: McGregor's UFC 329 return drew nearly 16 million viewers on Paramount+ across the US and Latin America.

cards.headline: NBA investigates Gary Trent Jr.'s $64m Bucks extension
cards.summary: The NBA is looking into the circumstances surrounding Gary Trent Jr.'s $64 million contract extension with the Milwaukee Bucks, with salary cap circumvention allegations emerging as soon as the deal was reported. The league has raised eyebrows over the structure of the contract. No formal findings have been announced yet.

quiz.prompt: How much is Gary Trent Jr.'s contract extension with the Bucks reported to be worth?
quiz.explanation: The NBA is investigating Gary Trent Jr.'s $64 million extension with the Milwaukee Bucks amid salary cap circumvention allegations.

cards.headline: US launches sixth straight night of Iran strikes
cards.summary: The US has carried out its sixth consecutive night of strikes on Iran, with Iranian media reporting airports, bridges and a communications tower were targeted. Iran condemned one attack near a children's cancer hospital as a 'barbaric war crime', while Tehran warned of a wider war. The US also boarded a ship in the Strait of Hormuz in an apparent escalation of the conflict.

quiz.prompt: How many consecutive nights of US strikes on Iran had occurred according to the reports?
quiz.explanation: Al Jazeera reported the US had mounted its sixth straight night of attacks on Iran.

cards.headline: Venezuela earthquake death toll nears 5,000
cards.summary: The number of confirmed dead from the Venezuela earthquake has risen to nearly 5,000, according to Al Jazeera. The UN estimates 50,000 people are missing, with many more feared buried under rubble. The focus is now shifting from rescue operations to rebuilding.

quiz.prompt: How many people does the UN estimate are missing following the Venezuela earthquake?
quiz.explanation: The UN estimates 50,000 people are missing in the aftermath of the Venezuela earthquake.

cards.headline: Canadian wildfire smoke blankets 20 US states
cards.summary: Smoke from more than 800 Canadian wildfires has spread across 20 US states, triggering hazardous air quality alerts from Minnesota to New York and affecting cities including Toronto. Orange skies and heavy smoke prompted authorities to urge people to stay indoors. Footage also showed wildfire smoke shrouding the New York New Jersey Stadium ahead of the World Cup final.

quiz.prompt: Approximately how many Canadian wildfires were burning according to reports?
quiz.explanation: BBC reported smoke from more than 800 blazes had affected cities from Toronto to New York and the US Midwest.

cards.headline: Trump delivers primetime address on election integrity
cards.summary: President Trump delivered a live primetime televised address focused on election integrity, having teased 'really big news' in advance. Critics, including a Democratic senator, said the speech was likely to be further 'election denialism' from the 'world's most famous sore loser'. Trump has long sowed doubt about the security of American elections.

quiz.prompt: What topic did Trump's primetime address focus on?
quiz.explanation: Multiple sources reported Trump's primetime address was focused on election integrity, a subject he has raised repeatedly since leaving office in 2021.

cards.headline: White House teleprompter operator suspended over alleged bets
cards.summary: Trump's longtime teleprompter operator has been placed on administrative leave after officials began probing whether he profited by betting on prediction markets based on advance knowledge of the president's speeches. Press secretary Karoline Leavitt called the alleged bets 'deeply unfortunate and frankly a disgrace'. It is described as the first known instance of suspected insider trading on a prediction market from inside the White House.

quiz.prompt: What platform were the alleged bets reportedly placed on?
quiz.explanation: The Guardian reported the alleged bets were placed on the Kalshi prediction platform.

cards.headline: FIFA probes Argentina's Falklands banner at World Cup
cards.summary: FIFA says its disciplinary committee is assessing match reports after Argentina players held a banner supporting their country's claims to the Falkland Islands following the World Cup semi-final. The governing body said it would consider relevant circumstances before taking any action. Argentina beat England in the semi-final to reach the final.

quiz.prompt: What did the Argentina players' banner relate to?
options: Argentina's claims to the Falkland Islands
quiz.explanation: Both BBC and Al Jazeera reported that FIFA is probing a banner held by Argentina players expressing support for their country's claims to the Falkland Islands.

cards.headline: Ebola spreading faster than any previous DRC outbreak
cards.summary: The WHO has warned that Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo has 'expanded faster than any previous outbreak' in the country. Meanwhile, Uganda has discharged its last remaining patient and is close to being declared virus-free. The diverging situations underscore the uneven regional spread of the disease.

quiz.prompt: Which country discharged its last Ebola patient and is close to being virus-free?
quiz.explanation: Al Jazeera reported Uganda discharged its last remaining Ebola patient and is close to being declared virus-free, while the DRC situation continues to worsen.

cards.headline: Trump tightens visa rules for foreign students
cards.summary: The Trump administration is tightening visa rules for international students, requiring federal permission for any student who wishes to stay in the US longer than four years. Universities will also lose their existing power to extend student visas. The move represents a significant shift in how foreign students' immigration status is managed.

quiz.prompt: Under the new rules, who must grant permission for a foreign student to stay in the US beyond four years?
quiz.explanation: BBC reported that under the new Trump administration rules, students need federal permission to remain in the US longer than four years.

cards.headline: Iran warns of wider war, eyes Red Sea disruption
cards.summary: Iran has warned of a wider war as US strikes continue, and the IRGC has signalled it could next target the Bab al-Mandeb strait in the Red Sea, after earlier threats to disrupt the Strait of Hormuz. Qatar rejected Israeli media reports claiming it had agreed to participate in military action against Iran. Analysts note Washington's spoiler problem also lies within its own alliance.

quiz.prompt: Which waterway did Iran's IRGC warn it could target after the Strait of Hormuz?
quiz.explanation: Al Jazeera reported Iran's IRGC warned it could next target the Bab al-Mandeb strait in the Red Sea following earlier threats to the Strait of Hormuz.

cards.headline: Burnham set to become UK Labour leader and PM
cards.summary: Andy Burnham is due to become Labour leader on Friday, with a speech setting out his vision for government before he becomes prime minister the following week. Outgoing PM Keir Starmer is stepping down as Labour leader Friday and as prime minister on Monday. Starmer's final honours list includes outgoing London Mayor Sadiq Khan and 25 others elevated to the House of Lords.

quiz.prompt: When is Andy Burnham expected to become prime minister?
quiz.explanation: BBC and Sky News reported Burnham will become Labour leader on Friday and prime minister the following Monday, when Starmer steps down.
